Clean Code in 10 Minutes — Every Key Lesson with Examples for Engineers: Robert C. Martin’s classic, distilled into the rules that actually change how you write code — with before/after examples for each one.Any fool can write code that a computer can understand. Good programmers write code that humans can understand.
— Martin Fowler (epigraph in Clean Code)
Covered in this summary :

• Meaningful names

• Functions — small and single-purpose

• Comments — use them rarely

• Formatting matters

• Error handling

• Classes and SOLID

• Tests and TDD

• Don’t repeat yourself (DRY)

• The Boy Scout rule
Emergence — the 4 rules of simple design
1. Meaningful names
Names are the most read thing in your codebase. A good name answers: what is this, why does it exist, and how is it used — without a comment.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

RULES:
1.1 Avoid mental mapping
Don’t use i, j, k outside tiny loops. Reader shouldn’t have to hold a mental model.
//…
